name: FLOOR — Factory Operations Platform
short: A full-stack factory operations platform — worker-facing shop-floor app and CXO command dashboard — built on top of on-ground research with garment factory owners and floor managers in Kolhapur and Ichalkaranji.
problem: Garment factory owners and floor managers were running on whiteboards, WhatsApp threads, and tribal knowledge. Real-time production visibility didn’t exist; CXOs and supervisors operated from completely different versions of the truth.
opportunity: shop-floor app for workers and supervisors
CXO dashboard with live production state
willingness-to-pay tested before a line of code
decisions: Customer discovery before code — on-ground interviews in Kolhapur and Ichalkaranji.
Worker-side UX designed for low-friction entry, not feature richness.
CXO view stripped to the four numbers leadership actually used.
Architecture scoped so a single engineer could ship and iterate the platform.
experience: CXO command dashboard for real-time production visibility.
Data ingestion pipelines feeding both surfaces.
impact: Field-validated product hypothesis with real factory owners.
Demonstrable willingness-to-pay signal inside the garment manufacturing segment.
reflection: You can’t design a factory tool from a meeting room. The product lives or dies in the first ten minutes a floor manager actually uses it.
